preFix.title: Choosing cleaning products that reduce waste and chemical load
preFix.subtitle: Concentrated formulas, refillable bottles, and what you can make at home
preFix.excerpt: Most household cleaning tasks can be handled by three or four products: a general surface cleaner, a degreaser, a descaler, and a disinfectant. Concentrated versions reduce packaging by 70 to 80 percent compared to ready-to-use sprays. Refillable formats cut that further. A short list of products used well produces less plastic, fewer chemicals down the drain, and often a lower cost per clean.
preFix.sourceNotes: WRAP cleaning products packaging research. Environment Agency guidance on cleaning chemicals in wastewater (2023). Which? eco cleaning product comparison (2024). DEFRA Green Claims Code and guidance on cleaning product marketing.

content.text: The average UK household spends around 120 pounds per year on cleaning products and generates 15 to 20 empty plastic bottles. Most of those bottles are 95 percent water. Concentrated products and refill systems reduce the bottle count by 70 to 90 percent for the same cleaning performance.

A 500 ml bottle of concentrated washing-up liquid at 10x concentration makes 5 litres of working product when diluted. The packaging saving is clear. The challenge is dilution: concentrated products used undiluted are wasteful and expensive, and may leave residue on surfaces.
Buy one spray bottle per product type and refill it with diluted concentrate. Mark the dilution ratio with a marker pen on the bottle.
Most concentrated surface cleaners dilute at 1:20 to 1:50. Check the product label. The cost per use drops sharply at higher dilutions.
General surface cleaner: diluted concentrate for worktops, bathroom surfaces, and sinks.
Degreaser: for oven, hob, and extractor fan grease.
Descaler: for kettles, showerheads, taps, and toilet bowls.
Disinfectant: bathroom contact surfaces and cutting boards after raw meat.
What you can make at home

Surface cleaner: 1 part white vinegar to 9 parts water in a spray bottle. Effective on most surfaces. Not suitable for marble, granite, or natural stone. Add a few drops of washing-up liquid for grease.
Descaler: neat white vinegar or a 10 percent citric acid solution. For a kettle: fill to mid-point, boil, leave for one hour, rinse twice.
Scouring paste: bicarbonate of soda mixed with a small amount of washing-up liquid to a paste. Works on sink stains and oven racks.
What home-made products do not replace

Vinegar and bicarbonate are not disinfectants. They do not kill norovirus, Salmonella, or other pathogens. For disinfection after illness, raw meat, or toilet cleaning, use a registered disinfectant at the labelled concentration. This is a small and specific part of the total product use, not an everyday cleaner.

initialFindings.message: tricolon: tricolon (three parallel items) — two adjectives almost always beats three
paragraph reads at grade 11.2 (qc threshold: 11) — simplify (target grade 6-8)
